nuggets.headline: jamee cornelia built an indie label from her old skate team's youtube needs
nuggets.text: Before becoming a solo musician, Jamee Cornelia got her start in the creative industry at 15 with a clothing company. This venture led her to become the videographer for her skate team, eventually becoming a go-to music video director in the Marietta and Atlanta area, which set the stage for her music career. Today, she co-runs the indie label 'L7 Records' with friends from her former hip-hop collective, Visibly Inflight, a name that harkens back to a store she wanted to open for her high school clothing company.

source.title: Conversations with the Inspiring Jamee Cornelia
source.quoteSnippet: I got into the creative industry at 15 with my own clothing company which was created to sponsor a skate team I was in. Eventually, we started needing photos and film lines for YouTube, so I became the videographer for the team... Currently, I still release solo music under Jamee Cornelia as well as direct music videos and me and the friends of the group I use to be in called Visibly Inflight run our own indie label “L7 Records” named after this store I wanted to open up for my clothing company back in high school at Marietta Square.

nuggets.imageCaption: in the Marietta and Atlanta area

nuggets.headline: jamee cornelia broke barriers as a queer black woman in a male-dominated industry
nuggets.text: Jamee Cornelia faced significant challenges early in her career, navigating a male-dominated creative field as a Queer Black Woman in the South. She often struggled to be taken seriously or paid her worth due to her youth, gender, race, and sexual orientation, particularly in areas like Cobb County. However, these same aspects became sources of strength, allowing her to flourish in East Atlanta and develop a distinct, authentic voice that resonates deeply with her audience.

source.title: Conversations with the Inspiring Jamee Cornelia
source.quoteSnippet: Well, the biggest bump for me is that I am a Queer Black Woman in a male-dominated field in the South. When I started, it was hard for me to get new people to want to work with me because I wasn’t taken seriously due to my youth gender race and at times sexual orientation. ESPECIALLY, when it comes to requesting someone to pay your worth. However, the same things that made me feel lesser than and made people treat me poorly in areas like Cobb County and where I’m from Fuquay Varina, North Carolina. I am FLOURISHING for in East Atlanta.

nuggets.imageCaption: Jamee Cornelia, a multi-talented artist from the Atlanta scene.

nuggets.headline: connect with jamee cornelia's atlanta scene through yani mo
nuggets.text: If you're digging Jamee Cornelia's sound, you should definitely check out Yani Mo. Like Jamee, Yani Mo is an integral part of the vibrant Atlanta hip-hop scene, known for her insightful lyrics and captivating performances. Both artists contribute unique perspectives to the city's rich musical landscape, making Yani Mo a natural next listen to explore the broader community Jamee Cornelia is a part of.

source.title: Conversations with the Inspiring Jamee Cornelia
source.quoteSnippet: I am FLOURISHING for in East Atlanta.

artistSummary: Jamee Cornelia is a multi-talented artist from Marietta, Georgia, known for her distinctive blend of Hip-Hop/Rap and Alternative Hip Hop. Beyond her solo music, she's a seasoned music video director and co-founder of the indie label L7 Records. Her music often delves into themes of love, heartbreak, and self-discovery, reflecting her journey as a Queer Black Woman in a male-dominated industry.
